learning_outcomes.description: Demonstrate an understanding of schools as workplaces and be familiar with the statutory, ethical and other requirements that govern the work of teachers in schools.

Demonstrate the application of professional and disciplinary knowledge to engage with curriculum, pedagogy, assessment and reporting of student outcomes, and use this knowledge to design lesson plans and learning experiences.

Create inclusive, supportive and intellectually challenging educational environments, use a range of resources including ICT and a range of teaching strategies that engage all learners, and set appropriate learning goals for students of all abilities and from a broad range of backgrounds.
Demonstrate a range of verbal and nonverbal communication strategies to support student engagement.
Demonstrate skills of critical reflection to evaluate student learning and assessment data, and make informed decisions regarding student learning and required modifications to teaching practices.

Demonstrate skills of and purpose for critical reflection and engagement with APSTs, colleagues and mentors to identify professional learning needs and develop achievable plans for personal professional growth. In doing so, effectively engage with constructive feedback through the stages of preservice teaching and beginning teaching.
Demonstrate clear, fluent, and coherent communication skills consistent with personal, professional and academic conventions.
Demonstrate all teaching and professional requirements of the professional experience placement.

assessments.task_description:
Students must read the Policies and Procedures for Professional Experience Placements document and respond to the statement declaration on Blackboard by the due date. Failure to do so will result in a withdrawal of the placement and the awarding of a grade of Fail for the course.

(APST: 7.1, 7.2)
assessments.submission_guidelines: Completion of declaration statement on Blackboard to acknowledge that the student has read, understood and will abide by the policies and procedures.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of this assessment will result in a failing grade
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Students are required to successfully complete 25 days (5 week block) of a supervised and assessed professional experience block placement in an approved school setting. The placement will be arranged only by the School of Education.

The 25 days (5 week block) will comprise of observations, hand-on classroom assistance, planning and teaching segments of lessons, full lessons and moving to sequences of lessons.

  • The first 2 to 3 days to be observation and settling into school environment and context, as well as planning and consulting with supervising teacher(s) for future teaching
  • Teaching segments of lessons towards end of Week 1 moving to full lessons start of Week 2
  • Sequences of lessons to start towards end of Week 3 until end of placement.
  • By the end of the placement students should be teaching 50% of a full-time teaching load
  • Preservice teacher observations continue throughout placement


During the practicum, students are expected to demonstrate competence across the APST. To do this, students will demonstrate that they have undertaken the inherent requirements of the role of a teacher. This means that students at the very least, will be expected to:

  • attend the school at least half an hour before and after school start and finish times to discuss that day's and subsequent days' expectations
  • attend any meetings held during the time of the practicum, e.g. departmental or whole of staff meetings or any professional development meetings where attendance is approved by the school,
  • participate in extracurricular activities etc available during the block.

Students are expected to have read, understood and will abide by the 2026 School of Education Policies & Procedures document for Professional Experience Placements.

(APST: 1.1, 1.3, 1.5, 2.1, 2.2, 2.3, 2.5, 2.6, 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 5.1, 5.2, 5.4, 6.3, 7.1, 7.2)
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without approved extension will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.assessment_title: Professional practice log and Reflective essay
assessments.task_description:
Part A – Professional practice log

During your five-week professional experience placement you are to keep a log of items collected, created, presented, and/or modified/adapted that could be used as evidence to demonstrate your fulfilment of Standards Three, Four and Five of Domain Two - Professional Practice. You are to use the attached professional practice log to record, annotate and map your chosen items against the standards and the specific focus areas. You are required to record 5 items - ideally you should aim to include items that address multiple focus areas and standards. Your annotation for each item should be approximately 150-200 words and should explain how the item demonstrates the identified focus areas (refer to the Graduate level descriptors to guide your annotations).

The chosen items must be attached as appendices to your professional practice log.

Part B – Critical reflection: impact on student learning

You are to critically reflect on how you were able to demonstrate a positive impact of your teaching on student learning during your five-week block. The focus on impact does not imply that you must show evidence of student growth on national or jurisdictional standardised tests. Rather the focus is on demonstrating the impact that you as a preservice teacher have had on student learning.

You are to prepare a 1000-word essay that critically reflects on the positive impact of your teaching on student learning. Evidence of the impact on student learning could take the form of a case study. You may choose to collect, interpret and use data sources/evidence to inform and then adjust your teaching to impact on student learning. This data may be from student, classroom, school and/or systemic levels. For example, you could analyse selected work samples from one student early and later in your professional experience block, or you could consult with one student of interest to inform your identification of learning needs and then again at the end of the placement to identify where learning or development has occurred.

It is recommended that some of the items collected in Part A should be linked to this task and should be the evidence you use to inform and prepare your critical reflection.

(APST: 3.1, 3.2, 3.3, 3.4, 3.5, 4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.4, 4.5, 5.1, 5.2, 5.4)
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without approved extension request via myUQ system will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: Personal development plan and critical reflection
assessments.task_description:
You are required to complete a personal development plan (PDP) to identify your professional learning needs so far in your teacher preparation program. Using the PDP template on the course Blackboard site, you are to evaluate your performance and knowledge against the Australian Professional Standards for Graduate Teachers. You should also refer to feedback provided by your supervising teacher(s) during your placement to assist your evaluation (e.g. lesson feedback, final evaluation).

Using your personal development plan, you are required to write a 1500 word critically reflective narrative where you reflect on your performance during the five-week professional experience block and identify where you need to improve your practice to better meet the learning needs of both yourself and your students. You should revisit the three professional learning goals that you identified in Primary Professional Experience 1.

You will also need to reference relevant literature that supports your analysis of your development and the ways in which you are seeking personal growth (e.g. consider theories on reflective practice, pedagogy, learning etc.). 

You should identify the ways in which your philosophy of teaching has evolved and changed since your first professional experience placement. You will also identify and explain the strategies that you will put into place before and during your final professional experience and how these strategies will enhance your practice.

(APST: 6.1, 6.3, 6.4)
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: Late submission of assessment without approved extension request via myUQ system will result in a failing grade.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing for professional practice

This lecture aims to clarify the legislative, school and employing authority requirements, and ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process.

We will cover the standards, legal issues, code of ethics, statutory obligations including occupational safety and child protection policies, as well as school visit protocols.
.


Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparing for professional practice This lecture aims to clarify the legislative, school and employing authority requirements, and ethical principles and practices in the teaching and learning process. We will cover the standards, legal issues, code of ethics, statutory obligations including occupational safety and child protection policies, as well as school visit protocols. .

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing for professional practice

In this workshop we will explore how you can best prepare for and make the most out of your professional practice experience. The focus will be on the practicalities of professional experience and establishing positive professional relationships with various stakeholders.

We will also further clarify the assessment requirements and available resources.
.


Learning outcomes: L01, L06, L07, L08
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparing for professional practice In this workshop we will explore how you can best prepare for and make the most out of your professional practice experience. The focus will be on the practicalities of professional experience and establishing positive professional relationships with various stakeholders. We will also further clarify the assessment requirements and available resources. .

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing for classroom management

In this lecture we will cover the key ideas related to fostering positive relationships in the classroom, and effective behaviour management strategies. The focuses will include creating safe and supportive environments, differentiation, organisation, establishing expectations, communication and observation.
.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparing for classroom management In this lecture we will cover the key ideas related to fostering positive relationships in the classroom, and effective behaviour management strategies. The focuses will include creating safe and supportive environments, differentiation, organisation, establishing expectations, communication and observation. .

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing for classroom management

In this workshop we will dig deeper into and practice specific strategies related to behaviour management. We will also engage in ongoing critical reflection, goal setting and planning to prepare you for success in professional placement and subsequent assessment requirements.
.


Learning outcomes: L01, L03, L04
learning_activities.topic_description: Preparing for classroom management In this workshop we will dig deeper into and practice specific strategies related to behaviour management. We will also engage in ongoing critical reflection, goal setting and planning to prepare you for success in professional placement and subsequent assessment requirements. .

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing for teaching and learning

In this lecture we aim to deepen your understanding of how teachers design learning sequences and lesson plans; and select and use appropriate resources across a range of subject areas. We will also explore using ICT to expand curriculum learning opportunities for students in ways that acknowledge and respond to issues of cyber safety, and ethical and responsible use


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06

We will also explore using ICT to expand curriculum learning opportunities for students in ways that acknowledge and respond to issues of cyber safety, and ethical and responsible use

learning_activities.topic:
Preparing for teaching and learning

In this workshop the aim is to prepare you for the practicalities of planning, implementing and reflecting upon lessons across a range of subjects. This includes planning to plan for the collection and use of ongoing assessment data to evaluate student learning and modify teaching practice. We will also engage in ongoing critical reflection, goal setting and planning to prepare you for success in professional placement and subsequent assessment requirements.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06

learning_activities.topic:
Reflection on the use of assessment in the classroom

This lecture will explore how assessment data within a primary context (systemic data, school, class) is used to evaluate student learning, provide feedback, and modify teaching practice.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Reflection on the use of assessment in the classroom
learning_activities.topic_description: Reflection on the use of assessment in the classroom This lecture will explore how assessment data within a primary context (systemic data, school, class) is used to evaluate student learning, provide feedback, and modify teaching practice.

learning_activities.topic:
Reflection on the use of assessment in the classroom

In this workshop you will critically reflect upon the various assessment strategies you implemented in the classroom while on placement. Including the use of diagnostic and summative assessment to guide initial planning, and the use of formal and informal formative assessment to provide feedback to students and modify teaching. You will continue to identify relevant areas for professional learning, growth and development.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L05, L06
learning_activities.topic_title: Reflection on the use of assessment in the classroom

learning_activities.topic:
Reflection on lesson planning and sequencing

In this lecture we will explore a range of planning and sequencing strategies, and how they might be influenced by teacher beliefs about the purpose of education. We will explore the similarities and differences between strategies implemented across different subjects, and for different epistemic aims.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03, L04, L06

Reflection on lesson planning and sequencing

In the workshop, you will critically reflect upon the teaching and strategies you implemented in the classroom while on placement. We will connect this to the previous week's exploration of assessment practices, and consider how you personally evaluated the success of these strategies. You will continue to identify relevant areas for professional learning, growth and development.

In this workshop, you will reflect upon your use of strategies and skills to maintain safe and supportive learning environments; support engagement; organise the classroom; and manage behaviour while on placement. You will continue to identify relevant areas for professional learning, growth and development. You will continue to identify relevant areas for professional learning, growth and development.

learning_activities.topic:
Reflection on supporting the strengths and needs of all students

In this lecture we will explore the breadth of factors involved when understanding the range of students a primary school teacher might encounter across their career. This includes understanding the needs of students of various backgrounds and abilities. There will be a focus on the interaction between a teacher's beliefs about inclusion and differentiation, and their associated practices in the classroom.
